title: Politically Inept With Homer Simpson

jokes.text: Finally. The only thing I wait in lines this long for are slightly better cell phones.
jokes.setup: Character expressing frustration about waiting in long airport line
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the modern obsession with minor phone upgrades
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about modern consumer culture, fits Lisa's character as someone who sees through societal absurdities

jokes.text: Why do we even have to go to this stupid wedding? / Cousin Kathy invited us so our feelings wouldn't be hurt. And we're going so her feelings won't be hurt. / I just don't understand the world of grown-ups.
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent confusion about the circular nature of polite social rituals
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart confusion about adult social conventions, well-structured with good character voice

jokes.text: Let me just redistribute our thingies amongst our various bags.
jokes.setup: Airport weight restrictions forcing bag reorganization
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual use of 'thingies' for important belongings
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor showing Homer's casual approach to organization

jokes.text: Don't argue in front of the airport line. Strangers are judging our marriage!
jokes.setup: Family having typical travel stress argument
jokes.punchline: Marge's self-conscious awareness of public judgment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge character moment - her neurotic awareness of social perception is perfectly captured

jokes.text: Lisa, do you really need all these Kurt Vonnegut novels? / They self-reference each other.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual justification for bringing multiple books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her intellectual pretension meets practical travel constraints

jokes.text: Marge, do we really need all these feminine products? / That's toothpaste! / Yes, but I never use that kind of toothpaste!
jokes.setup: Homer questioning Marge's packing choices with apparent gender comment
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually about his picky toothpaste preferences
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that starts seeming sexist but reveals Homer's petty preferences

jokes.text: Try to catch one on your tongue. / We're not with him.
jokes.setup: Homer making inappropriate suggestion about airport weight distribution
jokes.punchline: Family's immediate social distancing
jokes.explanation: Classic embarrassing Homer moment with family's predictable response

jokes.text: Now I have to change the nipple. / True patriots breastfeed.
jokes.setup: Airport security context with baby bottle
jokes.punchline: Marge's over-the-top patriotic declaration about breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd patriotic statement that could only come from post-9/11 airport paranoia

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! Can you focus on my shoulders? That's where I carry my stress. Now, if I fart, that's 'cause I'm so relaxed.
jokes.setup: Homer selected for airport security pat-down
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic acceptance and inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response to authority - turns violation into personal service with TMI warning

jokes.text: Due to an unforeseen jailbreak in New South Wales, our takeoff is going to be just a little bit delayed.
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd and geographically impossible reason for delay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on airline excuses - Australian prison break affecting US domestic flight

jokes.text: So, we're going to be turning off the air, but ethnic people are permitted to open and eat their home-cooked food.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant making announcements about discomfort
jokes.punchline: Casually discriminatory policy presented as a perk
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about casual airline discrimination, borderline offensive but fits satirical tone

jokes.text: Crazy food indeed, my friend.
jokes.punchline: Apu's dry acknowledgment of the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Apu's characteristic dry response to discrimination, good character voice

jokes.text: We've been cleared to taxi back to the terminal. You still can't get off, but you will be able to look through the window and see other people walking around and making fun of us.
jokes.setup: Airline update about being able to taxi back
jokes.punchline: Sadistic detail about being able to see freedom but not access it
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about airline passenger treatment - psychological torture presented cheerfully

jokes.text: But I have to go to the bathroom. / You should have thought of that before you drank the fluids you need to live.
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant treating hydration as poor planning
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd airline logic - blaming passengers for basic biological needs with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Don't tread on pee!
jokes.setup: Revolutionary War reference in bathroom emergency context
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'me' with 'pee' in famous patriotic slogan
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun on Revolutionary War slogan, fits Homer's crude humor

jokes.text: You make us beg for a filthy pillow that a barnyard rat wouldn't curl up and die in.
jokes.punchline: Vivid imagery of pillow quality being beneath even dying rats
jokes.explanation: Good escalating imagery in Homer's complaint, colorful metaphor

jokes.text: Look, all I want is what everybody wants: preferential treatment.
jokes.punchline: Revealing everyone actually wants unfair advantage
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about human nature - perfectly honest about universal selfishness

jokes.text: You can check my carry-on, but you can't check my spirit.
jokes.punchline: Playing on double meaning of 'check' - luggage vs. suppress
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that becomes a recurring catchphrase, good structure

jokes.text: And why is there moisture between the windows? And don't wake me up to land. Just land!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd demands about landing procedures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of complaints from reasonable to absurd

jokes.text: I regret nothing! Except this part.
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal when consequences hit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cowardice reveal - perfect timing and character consistency

jokes.text: Looks like we're back to traveling on tramp steamers and produce trucks.
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact acceptance of increasingly primitive transport
jokes.explanation: Good callback to family's history of transportation disasters

jokes.text: I call shotgun! / It's too early to call shotgun. / It's never too early to call shotgun. / No one's shotgun!
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument about timing rules with parental shutdown
jokes.explanation: Standard family bickering comedy, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: "Fatso Goes Nutso." And post to YouTube with the following key words: "fat," "stupid," "classic Simpsons," and just to be safe, "baby rides kitten rides penguin."
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on YouTube SEO tactics and viral video algorithms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on viral video culture and algorithm gaming, very current for 2012

jokes.text: Well, the hot dogs spin counterclockwise in fear whenever you are near, sir.
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation involving fearful hot dogs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Apu observation - poetic language applied to ridiculous concept

jokes.text: When I heard Homer speak, I no longer felt small.
jokes.punchline: Treating his tantrum as inspirational leadership
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how outbursts get misinterpreted as leadership

jokes.text: The quiet Homer Simpson that kept it all bottled up inside is dead. This is the birth of Homer Simpson, blowhard.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on his viral fame
jokes.punchline: Self-aware declaration of his transformation into loud pundit
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment with self-awareness of his new persona

jokes.text: your video has been mashed-up, Auto-Tuned, Jimmy Falloned, Philippine prisonered, and occasionally even watched.
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with joke that original viewing is rare
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about viral video remixes overshadowing original content

jokes.text: what we don't need is another blustering bloat bag who claims to speak for Bubba and Britney Spray-Cheese.
jokes.punchline: Condescending names for average Americans including product placement parody
jokes.explanation: Good satire of elite condescension toward average Americans

jokes.text: Now, I may not be some mani-pedied TV blowhard, but I do speak for the common man who does his lousy job, goes to church twice a year and watches women's tennis 'cause he likes to hear them grunt.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his credentials as common man
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and crude description of average guy behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer populist speech - escalates from reasonable to inappropriate with perfect character voice

jokes.text: Those real people out there who buy their coffee from the mini-mart and grab enough sugar packs for a week. Honest, hardworking, sugar-stealing Americans!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to champion common people
jokes.punchline: Describing petty theft as patriotic behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from relatable behavior to celebrating minor theft as American virtue

jokes.text: From now on, when someone asks me, I'm going to say you are my father. / Aw, son. / Now I regret bad-mouthing you to that girl you liked.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing pride in his father
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately confessing to sabotaging Bart's love life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - ruins tender moment with confession of past betrayal

jokes.text: You are the kind of ill-informed gasbag this network cannot get enough of. We only have 11 now.
jokes.punchline: Admitting they actively seek out ignorant pundits
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of cable news networks deliberately hiring uninformed but loud personalities

jokes.text: Not to mention our liberal straw man. ♪ Oh, the plain and simple facts is ♪ ♪ I'd love to raise your taxes And make your children gay... ♪
jokes.setup: Network executive mentioning their liberal pundit
jokes.punchline: Literally singing conservative talking points about liberal agenda
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on how cable news creates caricatures - literal straw man singing right-wing fears

jokes.text: Your flyover Franks, your dirt-bike Daryls. / How can you refer to your audience that way? / Well, what you do is take a derogatory term and think of other words that start with the same letter.
jokes.setup: Executive using condescending terms for audience
jokes.punchline: Explaining the systematic process of creating demographic slurs
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary exposing how media creates condescending audience categories

jokes.text: Finally. But I want to do it my way: classy and sophisticated. / Welcome to Gut Check with Homer Simpson! Where the truth is served with a side of... in your face.
jokes.setup: Homer promising classy approach to his show
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivering crude, aggressive intro
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - promises sophistication, delivers exactly the opposite

jokes.text: Audio book read by Lenny.
jokes.punchline: Random choice of Lenny as narrator
jokes.explanation: Simple absurd detail - why would Lenny read Homer's audiobook?

jokes.text: I'm a loudmouth who says things you're afraid to say, but not racist things!
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he's not crossing the racism line
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how pundits try to seem edgy while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: Nebraska. A high school principal has decided that football is too dangerous, so he's replacing it with... soccer.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting news story about football safety
jokes.punchline: Treating soccer as a punchline/threat
jokes.explanation: Standard American anti-soccer sentiment - predictable

jokes.text: Are you ready for Irish announcers with lyrical accents? "Oh, that's a lovely touch. Oh, such a beautiful form."
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about soccer replacing football
jokes.punchline: Mocking soccer commentary style with exaggerated Irish accent
jokes.explanation: Standard sports culture clash comedy with accent work

jokes.text: If we lose football, we lose the blitz, cheerleaders, Rudys, Ochocincos, something for fat kids to play.
jokes.setup: Homer listing what America would lose without football
jokes.punchline: Ending with blunt assessment of football as fat kid sport
jokes.explanation: Good escalation ending with Homer's blunt honesty about athletics

jokes.text: Shoot the mailman! Shoot the mailman. / Is it a little weird how much he cries? / No way. When a guy who loves America cries, it makes him super straight.
jokes.punchline: Explaining crying as proof of heterosexuality and patriotism
jokes.explanation: Satire of how emotional displays get interpreted in political media

jokes.text: I'm already a Halloween mask! / That's not you. They just painted Shrek yellow. / It's still a great honor.
jokes.punchline: Lisa deflating his ego, Homer maintaining pride anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - maintaining pride despite obvious knockoff

jokes.text: Great question. I know you children have never seen your father cry before. / Marge, do we have any more gravy? / No, we're out. / Why? Oh, why?!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming children have never seen him cry
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking down over lack of gravy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer contradiction - claims emotional restraint while crying over condiments

jokes.text: People know I'm doing a character, like Stephen Colbert or Newt Gingrich.
jokes.punchline: Comparing real politician to obvious character actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire suggesting Gingrich is as much a character as Colbert

jokes.text: D'oh! Death to America! I mean, over here.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to direct attention on his show
jokes.punchline: Accidentally shouting terrorist slogan
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer slip-up - accidentally saying worst possible thing on TV

jokes.text: When March Madness spills into April, that's the gravy. When someone messes with you, and you invade the country that did it plus another one, that's the gravy.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to casual endorsement of military overreach
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from harmless sports reference to casual warmongering

jokes.text: When you stick your flag in the moon and say 'Now we're done trying,' that's the gravy!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing gravy metaphor with moon landing
jokes.punchline: Suggesting America gave up after achieving one goal
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about American complacency after major achievements

jokes.text: Um. not a big deal, but next time, can we use real gravy instead of brown paint? Not a big deal.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation they used paint, minimizing health concern
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reveal of TV production shortcuts with health implications

jokes.text: You know, symbols can often rile people up. The swastika, the New York Yankees logo.
jokes.punchline: Comparing Nazi symbol to baseball team logo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about polarizing symbols, with sports rivalry humor

jokes.text: Don't worry, sweetie. I think I know how to whip up an audience just short of a frenzy.
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring Marge about his influence
jokes.punchline: Claiming precise control over mob psychology
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Homer claiming sophistication in crowd manipulation

jokes.text: Just an innocuous little symbol, like a smiley face or like an I.O.U.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his gravy boat symbol
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to innocuous symbols, including an IOU
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive comparison including financial obligation as 'innocuous'

jokes.text: The gravy boat movement is spreading across the nation like a rumor about some kid and someone's mom hooking up at a high school.
jokes.punchline: Comparing to specific type of high school gossip
jokes.explanation: Funny comparison between political movements and teen gossip patterns

jokes.text: Then what percent above 100 are you behind me? / Um, none. / Only 100%?!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to express reduced support
jokes.punchline: Homer treating 100% as disappointingly low
jokes.explanation: Good mathematical absurdity - Homer's ego demanding impossible percentages

jokes.text: Now, Lisa, I'm an entertainer. And you can't entertain and inform at the same time. And if you're Access Hollywood, you do neither.
jokes.punchline: Building to Access Hollywood doing neither entertainment nor information
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with sharp media criticism - solid structure

jokes.text: And you can't entertain and inform at the same time. And if you're Access Hollywood, you do neither.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his approach to media
jokes.punchline: Throwing in dig at celebrity news show
jokes.explanation: Good media criticism with specific target - Access Hollywood does nothing well

jokes.text: Now, they're all excellent choices, so simply pick the white male candidate you prefer and we'll elect him.
jokes.setup: GOP asking Homer to choose nominee
jokes.punchline: Reducing choice to demographic requirement
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about Republican candidate selection process

jokes.text: Save me, ObamaCare!
jokes.setup: Christie collapsing from apparent health issue
jokes.punchline: Republican calling for help from program he opposes
jokes.explanation: Excellent political irony - conservative needing liberal policy in crisis

jokes.text: The great thing is, when they get in, they act like Republicans.
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about Democratic governance
jokes.explanation: Sharp political cynicism about party differences in practice

jokes.text: No one's voting Democrat while I can still draw a bow.
jokes.punchline: Nugent's dramatic threat using archery metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Nugent character voice - mixing hunting imagery with political threat

jokes.text: I made love on my honeymoon to your sweet music. / You're my man!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about intimate moments
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer TMI moment with celebrity worship

jokes.text: That's all right, she can munch on an antler. Antlers ain't meat.
jokes.setup: Nugent offering elk to vegetarian Lisa
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic about antler consumption
jokes.explanation: Good absurd hunter logic trying to accommodate vegetarianism

jokes.text: From the looks of that moustache, I'm guessing you're a tenured professor at the People's Republic of Berkeley. / Hey, the only left-wing thing about me is the way I write.
jokes.setup: Nugent making political assumption about Flanders
jokes.punchline: Flanders' literal interpretation about handwriting
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Flanders completely missing political implication

jokes.text: I bagged me a fat little badger. / Please, kind sir, have pity. / All I hear is 'chitter-chitter.'
jokes.setup: Nugent hunting scene with talking animal
jokes.punchline: Refusing to acknowledge badger's pleas
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about selective hearing in hunting context

jokes.text: Arise, Homer Simpson! / Huh? Who's there? / Mozart! / I am James Madison, sir.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused guess of Mozart
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer confusion between historical figures

jokes.text: Can we get some pizza on the way? / Everything's closed. / They sell some at the gas station. / I don't want gas station pizza.
jokes.setup: Homer in historical vision with Founding Father
jokes.punchline: Typical Homer food priorities disrupting profound moment
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Homer treating mystical experience like casual errand

jokes.text: So, how come your picture's not on money? / Actually, I'm on the $5,000 bill. / Do you give them out for fans? 'Cause I'm a fan.
jokes.punchline: Treating Founding Father like celebrity giving autographs
jokes.explanation: Good Homer treating historical figure like modern celebrity

jokes.text: What's this? / Some kind of bicentennial lemon party?
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent mischaracterization with unfortunate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Homer's innocent phrase with unintended adult connotations

jokes.text: This great nation is built upon the hard work of, well, as you would say, nerds. / Nerds! You want I should mess them up?
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately wanting to beat up intellectuals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectual response to lesson about intellectual founders

jokes.text: You are an embarrassment to the aristocratic slaveholders who forged this mighty nation.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning founders' slavery in moral lecture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical irony - moral authority citing morally compromised founders

jokes.text: No, no, John Hancock. I don't need insurance.
jokes.punchline: Confusing historical John Hancock with insurance company
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on insurance company named after founder

jokes.text: Sam Adams, I'm sorry I made fun of your winter ale.
jokes.punchline: Confusing historical Sam Adams with beer brand
jokes.explanation: Another brand confusion joke, less clever than Hancock one

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, Oscar Madison came to me and told me that politics are serious and important!
jokes.punchline: Confusing James Madison with Odd Couple character
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Mozart confusion, Homer's pop culture brain

jokes.text: "Springfield Colonial Village"? / What the...? You faked it!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering the truth about his vision
jokes.punchline: Family staged the entire founding fathers experience
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist reveal - family intervention disguised as supernatural experience

jokes.text: I did it just to mess with your mind. / That's what a play within a play is for!
jokes.punchline: Invoking high literary concept for simple prank
jokes.explanation: Good meta-theatrical reference from surprisingly sophisticated Bart

jokes.text: Well, if there's one thing I don't like being taught, it's a lesson!
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-intellectual stance against learning itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - rejecting the concept of learning anything

jokes.text: Not only will I endorse Ted Nugent, but I will call for an end to the direct election of senators! / Dad, no! / Really? That made sense? / Then I'm definitely doing it.
jokes.punchline: His accidental stumbling into actual constitutional issue
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Homer accidentally proposing real constitutional change

jokes.text: Not only will I endorse Ted Nugent, but I will call for an end to the direct election of senators!
jokes.punchline: Homer threatening obscure constitutional change
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific threat - Homer going constitutional on them

jokes.text: Come on, hurry up and cry so I can get elected and open up the San Diego Zoo for big game hunting.
jokes.setup: Nugent waiting for Homer's emotional display
jokes.punchline: Revealing absurd policy goal of hunting zoo animals
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd Nugent policy goal - hunting captive zoo animals

jokes.text: Maybe because, deep down, you know you don't believe in what you're doing. / Oh, my God, she's right. My lips will say anything, but my eyes know the truth!
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic realization about his insincerity
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment with Homer recognizing his own dishonesty

jokes.text: My ears are keeping their mouth shut.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his body parts' honesty
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors about ears and mouths
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay mixing body part metaphors

jokes.text: My fellow Americans, I am full of crap.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest confession of his fraudulence
jokes.explanation: Excellent moment of Homer honesty - perfect economy and powerful character beat

jokes.text: I knew you were, Dad. I always knew!
jokes.setup: Homer's confession of being full of crap
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic validation that he always knew
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Bart's unwavering faith in Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: Well, for one brief, glorious moment, I was almost president, sort of, not really.
jokes.setup: Nugent reflecting on his political moment
jokes.punchline: Progressively diminishing his actual proximity to power
jokes.explanation: Good deflating sequence about delusions of political influence

jokes.text: ♪ Eat mooses raw so their souls go into my soul ♪
jokes.punchline: Increasingly unhinged lyrics about consuming moose souls
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation perfectly in character for Nugent's extremism

jokes.text: ♪ Suck on that, Francis Scott Key ♪
jokes.setup: Nugent claiming to write new national anthem
jokes.punchline: Aggressive challenge to original anthem composer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with characteristically aggressive Nugent delivery

jokes.text: Great song, Ted! When's our next gig? Are we going to New York or...? / Shh! / Oh...
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's now part of Nugent's act
jokes.punchline: Nugent cutting him off, Homer's disappointed realization
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer delusion about his relationship with celebrity

tmdb_overview: After a video of Homer ranting goes viral, he gets his own political talk show; Homer endorses rocker Ted Nugent for the Republican presidential nominee.
